title: Cross-Attention Between Text and Image Features
concept: Cross-attention layers (callback to Unit 270's decoder cross-attention) let image features 'look up' relevant words from the prompt at each step.

exercises.prompt: When you read the word 'red apple' in a text-to-image prompt, which part of the picture should pay closest attention to the word 'red'?

options.text: The pixels forming the apple's surface
The pixels forming the background sky
No pixels need to pay attention to any word

exercises.explain: The apple's pixels should 'listen' most closely to the word 'red' so they know what color to become — that's the whole idea behind cross-attention!

cards.title: How Does the Image Know What You Typed?
cards.body: You type 'a blue cat wearing a hat' — and somehow every part of the picture ends up matching your words. How does the image 'know' which word belongs where? The secret is a clever trick called cross-attention, where each part of the image reaches out and checks the text for instructions.

cards.title: What Is Cross-Attention?
cards.body: Cross-attention is a way for the image-generating part of an AI model to 'look up' relevant words in your text prompt while it builds the picture. At every step of drawing, each patch of the image asks: 'Which words in the prompt matter most to me right now?' and then borrows information from those words.

cards.title: Like Checking a Recipe Card
cards.body: Imagine you're baking and keep glancing back at a recipe card to check what ingredient goes next. You don't reread the whole recipe every second — you just glance at the relevant line. Cross-attention works the same way: each part of the image 'glances' at the word in the prompt that matters most for that specific spot.

cards.title: Painting 'A Yellow Bird on a Green Branch'
cards.body: While generating this image, the pixels forming the bird's body pay strong attention to the word 'yellow,' the pixels forming the branch pay strong attention to 'green,' and the pixels near the branch's shape pay attention to 'branch.' Each region pulls in the specific word it needs, instead of mixing everything together randomly.

cards.title: Myth: The AI Reads the Whole Prompt for Every Pixel Equally
cards.body: It's not true that every pixel treats every word the same way. Cross-attention gives different pixels different 'attention scores' for different words — the pixels forming a hat pay more attention to 'hat' than to 'sky.' This targeted lookup is what keeps images matching prompts accurately.

cards.title: Self-Attention vs. Cross-Attention
cards.body: These two mechanisms answer different questions, and both matter in image generation.

left.text: Image parts look at OTHER image parts to stay consistent with each other.

right.text: Image parts look at the TEXT prompt to know what they should become.

exercises.prompt: What does cross-attention let image features do?

options.text: Look up relevant words from the text prompt
Delete random words from the prompt

exercises.explain: Cross-attention lets each part of the image reach into the text prompt and pull out the words most relevant to that part.

exercises.prompt: True or False: In cross-attention, every pixel pays equal attention to every word in the prompt.
exercises.explain: False! Cross-attention gives different weights to different words depending on what's relevant to each specific image region.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the sentence.
exercises.sentence: Cross-attention lets image features ___ relevant words from the prompt at each step.
exercises.explain: 'Look up' is exactly right — cross-attention is like each image part checking the text for the instructions that apply to it.

exercises.prompt: In Unit 270, we learned about decoder cross-attention in translation models. How is that similar to cross-attention in text-to-image models?

options.text: Both let one sequence (output) look up relevant info from another sequence (input)
Both only work with numbers, never words or images
Both were replaced entirely and are no longer used

exercises.explain: Just like a translation decoder looks back at the source sentence to pick the right word, image features look back at the text prompt to pick the right visual details.

exercises.prompt: A student says: 'Cross-attention means the image just copies the text word-for-word onto the picture, like stamping letters.' What's wrong with this explanation?

options.text: Cross-attention lets image regions weigh and borrow meaning from relevant words to guide what they become — it's not literally copying text as letters onto the image
Nothing is wrong — that's exactly what cross-attention does
Cross-attention only works on black-and-white images
Cross-attention happens only once at the very end of image generation

exercises.explain: Cross-attention isn't about literally printing words onto pixels — it's about each image region gathering meaning and guidance from the most relevant words so it can generate the right colors, shapes, and details, and this happens repeatedly throughout the generation process.
